title: Migration: Switching (WashAssist → WashCentral)
note: DOC-SOURCED (title-card / document-over-narration) lesson. Every fact is from the Migration FAQ, carried verbatim by the captions; NONE of it is observable in the demo tenant. Live probing (CDP 9224, own tab, 2026-07-11) confirmed there is NO migration/switch UI to walk: migration.html and admin-portal.html 404; settings-integrations.html and settings-portals.html render but contain zero migration/WashAssist/Admin-Portal content and zero switch/migrate/activate/cutover/confirm buttons. Because the production engine requires a real styled+built app page as the capture backdrop, this lesson runs over a neutral, honest backdrop — the WashCentral Academy dashboard (lms-home.html), which is literally the training surface and shows no client/financial/credential data — while the locked narration plays as captions and numbered step callouts index the 9 FAQ steps. It is a pure browse-only piece: NOTHING is ever clicked (there is no Switch/Migrate/Activate/Confirm control on screen to click or even guardrail), the cursor only rests in the neutral right-margin gutter, and two mustHold watches prove the page never navigates or mutates for the whole take. See RESIDUALS in the build report.

ops.note: start page identity before capture: the Academy dashboard (lms-home.html) is the neutral document-over backdrop; there is no migration UI in the tenant, so nothing here is a migration screen
backdrop fully rendered (no skeleton/spinner) before capture
backdrop intact: on lms-home, 'My Dashboard' breadcrumb, >=5 course cards, no idle dialog

read-only proof: nothing is ever clicked across all 9 steps — the backdrop never navigates. There is no Switch/Migrate/Activate/Confirm/Cutover control anywhere on screen (verified live: no migration UI exists in the tenant), so none is pointed at or guardrailed; the captions carry the FAQ facts.
mutation guardrail: lms-home holds its 'My Dashboard' identity and course-card set the whole lesson — proves nothing was created, switched, redirected, or changed on the tenant

settle in the neutral right-margin gutter (1890,540; probe-verified cp-shell background, non-interactive, right of the leaderboard column at x>1869), clear of the step badge (top-left) and the caption card (bottom-center). An ABSOLUTE point is used deliberately: the breadcrumb x-origin shifts with sidebar state (probed center 840-950), so an element-relative landing is not reliably neutral, whereas this fixed gutter point always is. The engine's default pointer origin (960,1079, bottom-center) keeps the first logged sample well off screen-center. The cursor never gestures at content.
neutral page-identity chrome ('Academy › My Dashboard'); the 9 step callouts anchor here as a step index, not as a claim about the element
keep-alive drift in the neutral right gutter (session-active + non-dead cursor); step 1 caption running
keep-alive drift (right gutter); step 9 recap caption
end state: still on the Academy backdrop, never navigated
end state: backdrop intact — nothing on the tenant was created, switched, or changed

camera.note: a single, barely-perceptible slow push over the body of the lesson — document-over feel, never a tour; no close-up because the backdrop carries no migration detail to zoom into
settle wide for the completion card

completion.stamp: Source: Migration FAQ · Verified against demo.washcentral.com
